AOS ERP System

Welcome

Pick a module below to get started — every tab is one tap away.

Voucher details

This deducts the same amount from this site's Main Cash Box at the same time, since Sub is funded out of Main.
Posts to the site's LYD cash box. Switch to USD to enter and post this voucher's amount in US Dollars instead — it will post to the matching USD cash box.
For reference only — no longer part of the serial number.

Voucher

Charge / expense lines

Statement period

Pick any date range you like — a day, a week, a custom period, a whole year — and generate it whenever you need it.
Pick a period above and click "Generate statement".

Cash Statement — physical count reconciliation

Pick a site and month. The book totals (Total Cash, Total Booked Expenses, Balance per Books) and the Open Vouchers total are pulled automatically from the Treasury ledger — only the physical bill/coin count and any prepaid/foreign-currency notes on hand need to be entered by hand, exactly like the paper form. The Overage/Shortage at the bottom is then calculated for you.
Pick a site and month above, load, enter the physical count, then click "Generate Cash Statement".

Treasury balances — live, as of today

Daily balances report — all sites, all cash boxes

Shows every cash box's opening, cash in, cash out and closing balance for each day in the range — LYD and USD sit side by side in the same table, no separate table per currency. Built automatically from the movements you add below, no re-typing balances.
Pick a date range above and click "Generate daily report" to see every cash box's balance, day by day.

Treasury cash boxes — Main & Sub (LYD + USD) by site

Each site is optional — fill in whichever site(s) you have data for and leave the rest blank. Click Save to store, or Generate treasury statement to build a printable statement combining every site. Viewing, generating, printing and exporting the statement stay available to everyone — only editing and saving cash box data requires admin.

Voucher exchange rates (separate from the box rate above)

These rates are only used to convert a USD voucher's total into its LYD equivalent for printing and for the Daily Movement Expenses report. Completely separate from the "Exchange rate" field above — changing one never changes the others. Admin password required to set or change either one, so staff raising a voucher can't edit them.

Buy rate — Payment vouchers

Used for Cash/Bank Payment, Petty Cash, Advance, Settlement, Journal, Refund, Pay Slip and Open Vouchers — any voucher where cash goes OUT.

Sell rate — Receipt vouchers

Used for Cash Receipts and Bank Receipt vouchers — any voucher where cash comes IN.

Treasury cash flow statement (printable)

Fill in whichever site(s) you have data for above, then click "Generate treasury statement".

Daily Movement Expenses

Every voucher you issue is posted here automatically, the same day, into the correct cash box — no re-typing. Business rule: at a site with a Sub Cash Box, every voucher is paid out of (or received into) the Sub Cash Box only; the Main Cash Box is never touched by an ordinary voucher — it only moves when you record an actual fund transfer yourself in the Treasury tab. A site with only a Main Cash Box uses that instead.
Or pick a period to see every movement recorded across a range of days (same site as above):

Open Vouchers & Pending Deposits

Open Vouchers (advances not yet settled)

Auto-listed from every "Open Vouchers - Main/Sub Cash Box" voucher issued for this site. Mark one Settled once its expense has been reconciled — this only changes its status on reports, it never touches any cash box balance (the cash already moved when the voucher was issued).

Open vouchers (manual entry, FYI)

For an open voucher that wasn't raised through New Voucher — e.g. one issued outside the system. Informational only, shows on this report only, and does not affect any cash box balance.

Pending deposits (FYI)

Informational only — e.g. salaries or receipts awaiting bank confirmation. Does not affect any cash box balance.
Pick a site and date above, then click "Show this day".

Passport database — current log (checked IN)

This log shows passports currently checked IN. Checking a passport OUT removes it from this log automatically; checking it back IN adds it back automatically. Use the checkbox to also see passports currently OUT.
#NamePassport No.NationalityJob No. (ID)Expiry DateStatusDate InDate OutNoteActions

Add passport

Passport movement log (daily record)

Use the "Check IN" / "Check OUT" buttons on the passport database above to record custody movements. Checking a passport IN or OUT is logged here automatically with today's date and time — every day's movements stay on record.
DateTimeNamePassport No.MovementNote

Manual Desert Pass Records — current log (checked IN)

A simple typed-in record book for desert passes issued outside the generator below (e.g. historical records, or passes issued by another office). This does not generate or print a pass — use "New Desert Pass" below for that. This log shows holders currently checked IN; checking a holder OUT removes them from this log automatically, and checking them back IN adds them back automatically.
#NamePassport No.NationalityProfessionDestinationValid fromValid toStatusDate InDate OutNoteActions

Add record

Desert Pass movement log (daily record)

Use the "Check IN" / "Check OUT" buttons on the records above to record when a desert pass holder leaves for site (OUT) or returns (IN). Every movement is logged automatically with today's date and time.
DateTimeNamePassport No.MovementNote

New Desert Pass

"Desert Pass" is a travel/site-access permit for the selected person. Generate it below, then print, save as PDF, or add a secure QR.

Batch-print Desert Passes (each person on a separate sheet)

Tick everyone who needs a pass for the same trip/period, fill the shared details, then generate one PDF with each person's pass on its own page.

Residence database

Full record of residence (إقامة) permits for staff at this site. Every record still shows here even once it is expired — use the Status filter to isolate expired or soon-to-expire residences.
#NameEmployee IDProfessionEmployerResidence Expiry DateStatusNoteActions

Add residence

Operation Management

Keep one master list of field employees (one-time setup, or import from Excel), then each day just mark who is on leave or absent/sick — everyone else is assumed Active. The daily manpower report, grouped by field/site with totals, is generated automatically in the AOS format. Use the Site / Field filter in each tab to view, export, or issue the Employees Master and Daily Report for one site at a time, or leave it on "All Sites" for the combined company-wide report.

Employees master list

#AOS IDEmployee NameNationalityProfessionSite / FieldCategoryActions

Add employee

Manage sites / fields

Employees are always assigned from this list, so site names stay consistent everywhere. Adding, renaming, or removing a site requires the admin password.
#Site / Field nameEmployeesActions

Manage categories

These are the categories available for employees. The four default categories (Client Site, Camp Support, Staff/Office, Visitor) drive the report's grouping order and can't be removed. Adding or removing a custom category requires the admin password.
#Category nameTypeEmployeesActions

Daily attendance

#AOS IDEmployee NameNationalityProfessionSite / FieldStatusReason (if not active)

Report preview

Manpower period report

Pick any period — a single day, a week, a month, a quarter, a year, or a custom date range — and get a combined attendance report for that whole period: total days Active / On Leave / Absent per employee, and the same field/site summary as the Daily Report, totalled across every day in the range.

Period report preview

Custom Lists

Simple tracking lists anyone can define here — a name plus a few fields — without touching any code. Once a list is created it appears below for every signed-in staff member immediately, with a plain add / edit / delete screen. Good for things like "a new Passports-style list" that don't need a custom calculation, PDF layout, or workflow of their own.

New list

Fields — each row becomes one column in the list. Choose a type per field.

List

Add row

Contracts

Every contract created here uses the exact same official design as the Ministry of Labour "Non-National Employment Contract" (نموذج رقم 2021/8) — just fill in the fields, then download as a PDF or print, ready to sign and stamp.
#EmployerEmployee (per passport)DateActions

New contract

Page 1 — cover
Page 2 — date & first party (employer)
Page 2 — second party (employee)

Word Sheet

A blank document you can type into directly, or upload an existing Word (.docx) or text file to continue editing — then download it back as a .docx, export a PDF, or print.

Excel Sheet

A blank spreadsheet grid — click a cell to select it, then type to overwrite it or press Enter/F2 (or double-click) to edit it in place; click-drag or Shift+Arrow to select a range. Upload an existing Excel (.xlsx) or CSV file, then download it back as .xlsx, export a PDF, or print. Formulas start with "=" and support + − × ÷, parentheses, cell references (A1), ranges (A1:A5), and SUM / AVERAGE / MIN / MAX / COUNT — e.g. =SUM(A1:A5) or =A1*B1.
A1

Downloadable Report

Generates a formal, letterhead report — voucher activity, live treasury balances, and a full balance-by-site table for your selected period & site — ready to print or save as PDF.
Click "Generate report" above once the dashboard has finished loading.

Food Cost, by site

Sets the shared product list, prices and sites used by both this tool and the Food Stock tool (now under the Stock tab in the nav bar) — Food Cost totals what each site used, while Food Stock keeps a running inventory of what each site has on hand. Everything is saved on this device and synced to your shared Google Sheet automatically in the background — use "Export to Excel" / "Download PDF" any time you need a file.

Step 1 — Product prices (all sites)

Step 2 — Sites

No sites yet — click "+ Add site" to create the first one.

Quantities used

Site total: 0.000 LYD

Step 3 — All sites together

Step 4 — Food Cost Statement (PDF)

Generates a formal statement — same layout as your vouchers and Cash Flow Statement — showing every site's cost and a grand total, ready to print or save as PDF.
Click "Generate statement" above once you've entered site quantities.

Step 5 — Save today's entry to the log

Food Cost normally tracks one running total per site. To build daily / weekly / monthly / quarterly / annual reports, save a dated snapshot here whenever you finish entering a period's quantities — it records each site's current quantities and cost under that date. You can then generate a report for any range below.
DateSiteTotal (LYD)Actions

Step 6 — Period reports (daily / weekly / monthly / quarterly / annually / custom)

Builds a cost report from the saved log entries above, for any period you need.

Period report preview

Submit a food order request any time you need one — there's no approval step, it's logged instantly and synced to the shared Google Sheet so every device can see it.

Food Stock, by site

Tracks how much of each food item is physically on hand at every site — separate from Food Cost's "quantity used". Prices are shared with the Food Cost tab (set them there, in Step 1). Sites are also shared — add or rename them from the Food Cost tab.

Stock on hand

Step 2 — Stock movement (in / out)

Log deliveries in and consumption/waste out — the item's quantity on hand updates automatically.
Total In: 0   Total Out: 0

Step 3 — All sites together

Step 4 — Food Stock Statement (PDF)

Same layout as your vouchers and Cash Flow Statement — a summary plus every item's quantity, price and value, for one site or all sites together, ready to print or save as PDF. Tick "Low stock items only" to build a statement that lists just the items at or below their reorder level.

Step 5 — Food Stock movement statement (any period)

Pick any date range — a day, a week, a month, a year — and generate an Opening / In / Out / Closing statement built from the movement log, in the same design as the Cash Flow Statement.
Pick a site and period above, then click "Generate statement".
Click "Generate statement" above once you've entered stock on hand.

Stock System

Add a site (warehouse, branch, store…) for this stock. Each site gets its own sheet of items (with colour/size, quantity, unit price and reorder level) plus its own stock-movement log. Everything is saved on this device and synced to your shared Google Sheet automatically in the background — use "Export to Excel" any time you need a workbook with an Items sheet and a Movement sheet per site, plus a summary.

Step 1 — Stock sites

No sites yet — click "+ Add site" to create the first one (e.g. Main Warehouse, Tripoli Branch).
Import reads a workbook with columns like Item / Colour / Size / Quantity (any order, header row anywhere on any sheet) and adds them as items under the site you choose — matching cells left blank (merged cells) are carried down from the row above, just like the source sheet.

Items

Stock value: 0.000 LYD

Subtotal by item — same item, all colours/sizes combined

Groups every colour/size of the same item name into one subtotal line — e.g. all "T-Shirt" colours added together — for this site only.

Step 2 — Stock movement (in / out transactions)

Log every time stock comes in or goes out for this type. Pick an item to have its quantity update automatically, or leave it as "Not linked to an item" to just keep a note — the same way the original movement sheet worked.
Total In: 0   Total Out: 0

Step 3 — All stock sites together

Step 4 — Stock movement statement (any period)

Pick any date range — a day, a week, a month, a year — and generate a statement in the same design as the Cash Flow Statement, built from the movement log above.
Pick a stock site and period above, then click "Generate statement".

Step 5 — Full Stock Items Report

See every item currently in stock, any time you need it — for one site or all sites combined — as a formal, letterhead report with quantity, price, stock value and low-stock flags. (To add items in bulk, use "Import items from Excel" in Step 1 above.)
Leave "As of date" blank for the live snapshot of stock as it stands right now. Pick a past date to reconstruct quantities as of that date from the stock-movement log — this is only fully accurate for movements that were linked to an item (Step 3), so treat it as an estimate if free-text movements were used too.
Pick a stock site above (or leave "All stock sites"), then click "Generate report".

Live rates are fetched from a public exchange-rate service and are indicative only — always confirm against your bank or treasury rate before issuing a voucher. If the device has no internet access, type a rate manually in the field above.

Translator

Type or paste text and it translates automatically as you type — no need to click anything. Works right in the browser — nothing is saved or shared.
Arabic English
0 characters
Powered by a free public translation service — best for a paragraph or two at a time. Translates automatically a moment after you stop typing; click "Translate now" for an instant re-check.

Serial number format

AOS-TRP-DDMMYY-00001

AOS — fixed company prefix  ·  TRP — 3-4 letter site code, one per site (see Settings > "Manage sites")  ·  DDMMYY — the voucher's date  ·  00001 — a 5-digit running sequence number, scoped per site per calendar month (each site starts counting from 1 on its own).

Sequence range for a site & month

Configured site/month ranges

Connection diagnostics

If saving vouchers fails, use this to see the real reason instead of a generic message.

Staff accounts (admin only)

Add, remove, or change the name/password each staff member uses to sign in. Protected by a separate admin password.

Login history (admin only)

Every sign-in — who, account type, site, and the exact date & time — is recorded automatically the moment someone signs in, and synced to its own tab on the shared Google Sheet, the same way vouchers are. Protected by the same admin password as Staff accounts above.

Pick a date range above (staff member is optional) and click "Generate report" — defaults to today, so it's ready for a same-day report with one click.

Manage sites (admin only)

Add a new site, relabel an existing one, toggle which cash boxes it has, or deactivate one you no longer use — every device picks this up automatically, no new file or redeploy needed. Sites are never deleted here: deactivating only hides a site from new-voucher/new-staff dropdowns, all of its historical vouchers and reports stay exactly as they are. A site's 3-4 letter code locks automatically once any voucher has been issued under it (changing it would break already-printed serial numbers) — deactivate it and add a new site instead if you need a different code. Protected by the same admin password as Staff accounts above.

App settings (admin only)

Change passwords, cash-count denominations, company branding, a couple of thresholds, and which tabs are shown app-wide. Saved here, these apply automatically on every device the next time it loads or refreshes the app — no new file to send around. Protected by the same admin password as Staff accounts above. (Brand-new *kinds* of features with their own unique logic are still code, not data — see Code.gs for how those now reach every device too, via a single hosted link instead of a resent file.)

Company branding

Shown on the lock screen, the app header, and every printed voucher.

Thresholds

Tabs shown in the app

Untick a tab to hide it for EVERYONE, app-wide — on top of (not instead of) each staff member's own checklist in "Manage staff accounts" above. Useful for quietly retiring a module nobody uses anymore, without touching any code. "Settings" can't be hidden this way, so nobody gets locked out.

Edit Voucher

Password required

Add New Product

Adds a product to the shared price list — it will also appear in Food Stock and every site's quantity list. Price is optional here; you can always set or change it later in the price list.

Staff account management
